programName: Horizon Europe - HORIZON-CL4-2026-01 (Digital, Industry and Space)
summary: This Coordination and Support Action aims to enhance EU knowledge and strategic capacity for secondary raw materials through comprehensive monitoring, data collection, and analysis across multiple waste streams. The action will develop classification systems, monitor secondary raw materials flows within and beyond the EU, and create reports on future trends to strengthen EU autonomy in raw materials supply and support the transition to a climate-neutral, circular economy.
objectives: Enhance knowledge base of secondary raw materials in the EU and third countries, including their potential, resource estimation, production, refining processes and biodiversity footprint
Accelerate development of projects leading to commercial exploitation of secondary raw materials in the EU
Develop reports analyzing future trends in raw materials markets and identify supply and demand bottlenecks
Improve EU raw materials intelligence, strategic planning and foresight capacity
Develop transparent and objective classification approach for secondary raw materials using UNFC format
Create proposal for EU statistics for secondary raw materials
Monitor waste flows between Member States and wastes exported from Europe
Include biodiversity footprint assessments using natural capital accounting frameworks

requirements.description: Legal entities must be established in Member States, associated countries, OECD countries, African Union Member States, MERCOSUR, CARIFORUM, Andean Community, or countries with EU strategic partnerships on raw materials or trade agreements containing raw materials cooperation provisions
Proposals including legal entities not established in eligible countries will be ineligible
Entities directly or indirectly controlled by non-eligible countries or entities from non-eligible countries shall not participate
Actions must be based on common understanding of relevant terms and codes for anthropogenic resources classification
Acquire new data via in situ sampling from different regions across the EU and collect existing data in harmonized UNFC format
Focus on specific secondary raw materials streams: waste batteries, WEEE, mining waste, slags and ashes, and construction and demolition waste, with particular attention to critical raw materials
Include biodiversity footprint assessments using natural capital accounting frameworks
Monitor waste flows between Member States and wastes exported from Europe, focusing on countries where waste goes into European recycling industrial ecosystems
Anticipate the evolution of WEEE directive
Share all data and information in open formats on a free of charge basis with the European Commission
Include dedicated work package/task for clustering activities with other relevant projects and earmark appropriate resources
requirements.evidence: Work package dedicated to clustering activities

requirements.description: Comply with proposal page limits and layout requirements as described in Annex A, E, and Part B of Application Form
Demonstrate operational and financial capacity as per Annex C of Work Programme General Annexes
International cooperation encouraged with countries with EU Strategic Partnerships on raw materials, especially Ukraine
requirements.evidence: Partnership agreements or letters of support

requirements.description: Could build on work of UNECE-UNFC expert group on Anthropogenic resources and Horizon Europe project FUTURAM

evaluationCriteria.name: Excellence
evaluationCriteria.description: Quality and credibility of the proposed action, including quality of objectives, methodology, and work plan. Alignment with topic scope and expected outcomes.
keyIndicators: Soundness of methodology for classification and monitoring
Quality of data collection and harmonization approach
Credibility of biodiversity footprint assessment framework
Innovation in addressing secondary raw materials intelligence

evaluationCriteria.name: Impact
evaluationCriteria.description: Credibility of pathway to impact, contribution to expected outcomes, and measures to maximize impact including dissemination, exploitation, and communication.
keyIndicators: Contribution to EU raw materials intelligence and strategic autonomy
Potential to accelerate commercial exploitation of secondary raw materials
Quality of dissemination and communication plan
Contribution to EU policy development and statistics

evaluationCriteria.name: Implementation
evaluationCriteria.description: Quality and effectiveness of work plan, resources, and consortium composition. Appropriateness of management structures and procedures.
keyIndicators: Quality of consortium composition and complementarity
Quality of clustering and cooperation strategy
Effectiveness of management and coordination structures

categories.description: Lump sum grant for coordination and support action
rules: Eligible costs will take the form of a lump sum as defined in the Decision of 7 July 2021
Standard lump sum model for Horizon Europe CSA applies

categories.description: Dedicated resources for cross-project cooperation, consultations, joint activities, meetings and communication events
rules: Must be earmarked in dedicated work package and/or task

timeline.duration: As per indicative timeline in Annex F
As per indicative timeline in Annex F

deliverables: Data collection from in situ sampling across EU regions
Classification framework for secondary raw materials
Proposal for EU statistics for secondary raw materials
Open format data sharing with European Commission

timeline.duration: Up to 4 years after end of action
deliverables: Granting authority may object to transfer of ownership or exclusive licensing of results

targetBeneficiaries: Waste management and circular economy actors
geographicScope: European Union with international cooperation components
eligibleCountries: Countries with EU strategic partnerships on raw materials (including Ukraine)
Countries with trade agreements containing raw materials cooperation provisions
additionalNotes: Single-stage submission process with deadline on 2026-04-21
Expected to fund 1 grant under this specific topic (HORIZON-CL4-2026-01-MAT-PROD-13)
Type of action: HORIZON Coordination and Support Actions (CSA)
Lump sum grant model applies - see Decision of 7 July 2021 for details
Destination: Achieving technological leadership for Europe's open strategic autonomy in raw materials, chemicals and innovative materials (2026-27)
Strong emphasis on data sharing and open access - all generated data must be shared free of charge with European Commission
Must align with Critical Raw Materials Act (Regulation EU 2024/1252)
Should build on UNECE-UNFC expert group work and Horizon Europe project FUTURAM
Could leverage knowledge from projects funded under HORIZON-CL6-2021-BIODIV-01-15
Special attention to avoiding reinforcement of existing over-dependencies
Security provision: entities controlled by non-eligible countries cannot participate even if established in eligible country
International cooperation particularly encouraged with Ukraine and countries with strategic raw materials partnerships
Must use UN WCMC natural capital accounting frameworks for biodiversity assessments
Application form: Standard HE CSA form available in Submission System
Model Grant Agreement: Lump Sum MGA applies
